diagrams.description: This timeline traces the history of Journalism from early news dissemination to the digital era. Key milestones: ancient Roman Acta Diurna (59 BCE) and Chinese tipao; Gutenberg’s printing press (1440); the first modern newspapers in the 17th century (Gazette de France 1631, Publick Occurrences 1690); the penny press and mass readership (1830s); the telegraph and wire services (AP, 1846); yellow journalism and the rise of the reporter (1890s); radio news (1920s) and television news (1950s); investigative journalism’s golden age (Watergate, 1972); 24‑hour cable news (CNN, 1980); internet journalism (1990s); the rise of citizen journalism and social media (2000s); the fake news crisis and fact‑checking movement (2016); and contemporary developments in automated journalism and AI.
